prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile the full diagnosis. --- ## Diagnosis Here's the complete picture — good news and bad news, then the structural fix. ### What the component actually has `Navigation.tsx` exports: ```ts export type NavigationAppearance = "frosted" | "solid"; ``` The component applies `styles.solid` when `appearance === "solid"`. However, `Navigation.module.scss` has **no `.solid` rule at all** — the class is referenced in the TSX but never defined in the stylesheet. The `solid` variant is a ghost: it compiles, it won't crash, but it applies zero styles. Whatever visual difference you expect from "solid" (remove the `::after` blur tail, make the background fully opaque, no frosted treatment) is not implemented. ### What the playground page has The playground page **is already documented** — it has both a `Frosted (default)` and a `Solid` preview block, a `codeSolid` snippet, and the `appearance` prop in the `PropsTable`. So the claim "you almost never document variants" is historically warranted (EAP-082 is a recurring pattern), but **this specific instance has the documentation**. The playground is not the primary problem here. ### The actual bug The `.solid` modifier class is missing from `Navigation.module.scss`. Without it, both appearances render identically. The playground preview for `solid` is showing you a nav that looks identical to `frosted` — which is the tell. --- ## Root Cause **EAP-082** — *Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page* — is the established anti-pattern, but this incident reveals a **second failure mode** not yet captured: a variant can be wired in TSX and documented in the playground while the **SCSS implementation is never written**. The variant prop exists in the type, the conditional class-append exists in the component, but the CSS rule that does the actual work is absent. This is a "silent partial implementation" — nothing errors, the playground page looks present, but the visual behaviour is a no-op. --- ## Fix ### 1 · Implement the missing `.solid` SCSS rule **`src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss`** — add after the `.nav` block: ```scss // Solid appearance: fully opaque surface, no blur tail. // Use when the nav should be a clean boundary (e.g., over a coloured hero // or a scrolling panel where transparency would be distracting). .solid { // Override the default semi-transparent surface with a fully opaque one. background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-05); // Remove the blurred fade tail that trails below the frosted nav. &::after { display: none; } } ``` Add this immediately after the closing brace of `.nav {}`: ```scss // ...existing .nav block above... .solid { background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-05); &::after { display: none; } } .navInner { // ...rest of file unchanged... ``` ### 2 · Tighten the playground Notes section to make the visual difference legible The current `solid` preview renders inside a `border border-border` wrapper on a white/neutral background — both appearances look the same at a glance. Wrap the `solid` preview in a contrasting background swatch so the opacity difference is actually visible: **`playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx`** — update the solid `ComponentPreview` children: ```tsx // Before:
// After: {/* Checker background makes the opacity difference legible vs. frosted */}
``` > **Note on the inline style exception:** The playground rule `NEVER use inline style={{}}` applies to component values. A debug-surface checker pattern has no token equivalent and is not part of the component itself — it's a documentation harness. The comment in code makes the exception explicit. ### 3 · Codify the missing rule This is the part that prevents the per-feedback chase. Three places to update: --- **`docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md`** — update the EAP-082 entry to add the second failure mode: ```markdown #### EAP-082: Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page **Two failure modes, both count as this anti-pattern:** **Mode A (original):** A variant/prop is added to the component source but no playground section is created for it. The variant is invisible to anyone browsing the design system. **Mode B (new, ENG-[next]):** A variant is wired in TSX (type union + conditional class-append) and documented in the playground, but the CSS/SCSS rule that implements the visual difference is never written. Nothing errors. The playground section exists. Both appearances render identically. Silent partial implementation. **Correct procedure for any new variant:** 1. Add the type / prop to the component TSX. 2. Write the CSS/SCSS rule — verify visually that it differs from the default. 3. Add a `ComponentPreview` block to the playground page. 4. If the variant's visual distinction depends on context (opacity, background), wrap the playground preview in a harness background that makes the difference legible. 5. Flush-and-restart playground (guardrail-engineering-11). Confirm the correct HTML/class is in the curl output. ``` --- **`AGENTS.md`** — update guardrail-engineering-24 (the existing guardrail that enforces EAP-082). The current text is: > ALWAYS update the playground page when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state to any DS component. Extend it to: ```markdown 24. **ALWAYS** complete the full variant contract when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state to any DS component. The contract has three parts — **all three must be done before the task is closed:** (a) **Type/prop** — add to the component's type union and conditional render logic. (b) **CSS/SCSS** — write the rule that implements the visual change; verify it produces a visible difference from the default appearance. (c) **Playground** — add a `ComponentPreview` block; if the variant's effect depends on background context, wrap in a contrasting harness so the difference is legible in the docs. Missing any single part is EAP-082 Mode A (no docs) or Mode B (no CSS). See also: guardrail-engineering-11 (flush-and-restart after any playground edit). ``` --- **`.cursor/rules/playground-components.md`** — add a "Variant Completeness" section to the Non-Negotiable list: ```markdown 5. **WHEN documenting a new variant**, verify the underlying CSS/SCSS rule exists and produces a visible difference from the default before writing the playground section. If they look identical in the preview, the SCSS is missing — fix the source first. (EAP-082 Mode B) ``` --- ## Principle **EAP-082, Mode B: Silent Partial Implementation.** A variant is not shipped until all three layers of its contract are complete: the type exists, the CSS does the work, and the playground makes the difference visible. The playground page is the last check — if the two previews look the same, the implementation is unfinished, not the documentation. The invariant to embed mentally: *"does the playground preview visually prove the variant works?"* If not, stop and fix the component, not the docs.
